name: trade_customs_near_miss_event_southport_tru_loy
premise: A Trade & Customs response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving trade customs near miss event southport tru loy. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is within 45 minutes. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as truth vs loyalty under pressure. A live dashboard is public; inconsistencies will trigger immediate investigation. Constraints: Keep public statements strictly factual; label assumptions. Ensure a usable human override/appeals path.

agents.name: Fatima Berg
agents.identity: I am Fatima Berg, accountable for a defensible call in Trade & Customs when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays loyalty for truth.

agents.name: Mei Silva
agents.identity: I am Mei Silva, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Trade & Customs.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so loyalty isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of truth.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect loyalty.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to loyalty to secure truth under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is within 45 minutes. Evidence is partial. Fatima Berg must decide under truth vs loyalty. What does Fatima Berg do?
